title: I can't seem to use samba with Win10 network discovery in podman rootless.
body:
https://github.com/ServerContainers/samba
https://github.com/Netgear/wsdd2

wsdd2 listens to the UDP port 3702 of the broadcast address 239.255.255.250 to work, and I seem to have to use the host network mode.
But samba can monitor ports 139 and 445 to work, but these two ports are privileged ports of Linux, and it seems that they cannot listen in rootless mode.
In addition, my commonly used firewall NAT method of mapping privileged ports to non-privileged ports is invalid because the host network mode discards the port binding settings.
It seems that I can only modify the default listening port of samba to an unprivileged port so that samba and wsdd2 can work normally.
Is there a way to enable podman in rootless mode to listen to multicast addresses? And the host network mode seems to be insecure.

comments.body: The only way I can see this working is if you reduce the number of the maximum privileged port to allow rootless Podman to bind to ports under 1024 - this is done via the `net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start` sysctl
It seems that the safety is reduced to a considerable extent.

The only risk is a rogue processes running in non root on the system listening on ports > 139 impersonating something that a remote user trusts to be only run as root.
I don't see this as a huge security risk, as long as you control what runs on this system.

Due to the large number of vulnerabilities in the smb protocol in the past and possible zero-day vulnerabilities, running the smb protocol with too high permissions makes me panic. I can only abandon the wsd-based network discovery and make the container work in rootless mode.

> The only way I can see this working is if you reduce the number of the maximum privileged port to allow rootless Podman to bind to ports under 1024 - this is done via the `net.ipv4.ip_unprivileged_port_start` sysctl

> It seems that the safety is reduced to a considerable extent.

You could reduce the number of the maximum privileged ports before launching your podman node. And then set it back to normal.
